Define MCP tool-call success metrics spec
Extract first handler from large file

doctor command help + docs section + tests
Implement coverctl mcp doctor First-Run Validation
Reframe landing Problem + Solution sections
tasks.description: Rewrite docs/src/content/docs/index.mdx 'The Problem' and 'The Solution' sections around agent-loop struggling moment. Replace 'Traditional coverage tools give you one number for your entire codebase' (Codecov-frame critique) with the red-CI agent loop status quo from docs/strategy/category-pov.md. Replace YAML config snippet in 'The Solution' with the agent-loop solution flow (agent calls check/suggest/debt mid-edit). Keep YAML further down for reference.

tasks.title: Replace generic Cards + cut Designed for Teams
tasks.description: Replace landing's generic feature Cards (Domain-Aware Policies / Multi-Language Support / CI-Ready / Auto-Discovery) with agent-loop-anchored value props that name the wedge: 'Catches regressions before commit' / 'Agent-callable via MCP' / 'Polyglot governance, one config' / 'Local-first, no SaaS'. Cut the 'Designed for Teams' section entirely — bullets restate product without selling outcome and the DDD-principles language collides with the coverage 'domain' concept.

tasks.title: Name the category and repeat the phrase
tasks.description: Promote 'agent-loop coverage governance' as a named category phrase to README hero, landing tagline, and the first sentence of every blog/conference pitch. Rule (Lochhead): until visitors can repeat the phrase, category design isn't working. Land in: README hero sub-line, landing tagline, README first paragraph, mcp.mdx intro paragraph. Add explicit alternatives block (red-CI agent loop status quo / dashboards / native commands) under the hero on both surfaces — sets comparison frame before visitor sets it.

tasks.title: Tighten hero density + remove copy leaks
tasks.description: README: compress over-feature-densified sub-line ('Domain-aware policy, fifteen languages, no SaaS account, no source upload' → one differentiation phrase 'Local-first, MCP-native'); drop 'first-class' modifier; replace 'designed with domain-driven design principles' bullet (architectural leak collides with coverage-domain concept); contextualize 'local-first' to customer benefit. Landing: trim hero CTAs from 3 to 2 (drop GitHub button, available in nav); drop Languages badge (claim already in tagline).

tasks.title: Validate transcript against real Claude Code session
tasks.description: Before publishing the on-page agent transcript: run the exact prompt ('Add the email-validation feature to the api domain') through a real Claude Code session against a fresh repo with coverctl wired in. Capture the actual interaction. Compare against ai-expert's idealized transcript. Decide: clean up the on-page version with a small 'rendered for clarity' note, or use the raw real one. Either is honest; pretending the idealized one is real loses trust on first install.

tasks.title: Add transparency-moment artifact to README
tasks.description: Insert immediately after the hero/sub-hero, before any feature catalogue: (a) 6-line agent-session transcript per ai-expert's concrete copy showing tool calls inline, structured rejection, chained suggest call, agent's natural-language plan with explicit failing domain + uncovered lines; (b) anti-pattern card with 3 hallucination markers (lowering thresholds as 'fix', claiming coverage rose without a new check, ignoring error_code on retry); (c) compressed agent-calibration block (does well / watch for / contract is in docs).

tasks.title: Mirror transcript + calibration to landing
tasks.description: Add the same transparency-moment artifact (transcript + anti-pattern card + calibration block) to docs/src/content/docs/index.mdx between the hero and the value-props cards. Match formatting to Astro Starlight components (Code component for transcript, Aside for calibration). Keep README and landing in sync with a single source-of-truth fragment if Astro Starlight supports MDX inclusion.

tasks.title: Replace Security note with full Security Boundaries
tasks.description: Rewrite README 'Security note for MCP users' as 'Security boundaries' with two-bullet structure covering Lethal Trifecta framing: (a) Input boundary — sanitization rejecting dangerous flags (existing content); (b) Output boundary — canonicalization of user-controlled strings flowing back to agent (filenames, test names, profile-derived paths, PR descriptions in pr-comment). Link full threat model. Mirror compressed version to landing's footer or a dedicated security card.

tasks.title: Reframe MCP-native to agent-loop-native via MCP
tasks.description: Replace 'MCP-native, first-class MCP server' across README + landing with 'agent-loop native via MCP' framing plus explicit multi-vendor acknowledgment: 'Speaks MCP — the multi-vendor agent-tool standard now governed by Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, AWS. Works with every MCP-capable client today; works with whatever ships next without modification.' Addresses procurement vendor-lock-in question; repositions stale 'first-class' claim now that MCP is multi-vendor table stakes.

tasks.title: Privacy-first + eval-gated framing + compatibility
tasks.description: Add three-sentence privacy-first + eval-gated paragraph to README near security section: local-first default, opt-in --mcp-telemetry only, 50+ adversarial eval scenarios gating releases (link mcp-metrics-spec.md + internal/eval/). Consolidate compatibility surface single-source on both README and landing: 'Compatible with every MCP-capable client. Tested against Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Aider, Continue, OpenCode.' Eliminates README/landing inconsistency.

tasks.title: Add Community section to README
tasks.description: New section between Quickstart and CLI reference: Claude Code Plugin Marketplace link, MCP Registry link, GitHub Discussions link, GitHub Sponsors button, maintainer credit + contributor count. Six lines maximum. Surfaces primary motion (community-led) that has zero current surface area; opens sponsor path; signals 'real project, not vaporware.'

tasks.title: Add For Platform & DevEx Teams section
tasks.description: New section linking the artifacts Jordan-class buyers paste into security/procurement reviews: docs/security/mcp-threat-model.md, rejection schema in mcp.mdx, docs/design/gtm-metrics-spec.md. Add low-friction inbound path: 'Open a GitHub issue with label platform-evaluation' — auto-tags Stage 4 monetization trigger metric. Create the 'platform-evaluation' label on the GitHub repo as part of this task.

tasks.title: Pull-quote + PMF nudge + monetization framing
tasks.description: Three small high-leverage adds: (a) pull-quote ICP brief's customer struggling-moment line ('Our AI agents ship code fast, but they're blind to coverage policy while editing...') above the install commands per Gerhardt audience-first; (b) one-line PMF survey nudge under Community section ('Used coverctl for a few weeks? Run coverctl survey to share feedback'); (c) pre-emptive monetization framing in 'Why this exists' ('CLI and MCP server are Apache-2.0 licensed and stay free. A hosted layer for cross-repo coverage history is on the roadmap — additive, not a paywall.').

tasks.title: Reorder README first viewport + add nav strip
tasks.description: Reorder README so first scannable block is: hero → install line → MCP JSON config → agent-prompt example. Prose moves below. Add scannable nav strip near top: '[Get started] · [MCP tools] · [CLI reference] · [Configuration] · [Why this exists]'. Reorder full README sections: hero → install → transcript → why → MCP tool reference → CLI reference → configuration → community → security → contributing. Cuts visual hierarchy debt; matches developer README scan norm (Jakob's Law).

tasks.title: Collapse human quickstart + move Architecture out
tasks.description: Collapse 'Quickstart for humans' and 'Golden path (first 10 minutes)' sections into the CLI reference (they are how-to fragments, not first-touch). Move detailed Architecture section to a dedicated ARCHITECTURE.md (or below Contributing) — useful for contributors but not for first-read decision. README sections drop from 11 to 6.

tasks.title: Asymmetric Quick Start CardGrid on landing
tasks.description: Replace the symmetric two-LinkCard pattern in landing's Quick Start with asymmetric primary agent card + inline secondary terminal link. Agent path is full-width or 2x sized with rich preview; terminal path is a one-line link. Restores hierarchy the GTM decision already made (community-led primary motion, agent path is the wedge).

tasks.title: Trust calibration one-liner on both surfaces
tasks.description: Add one-liner immediately after the wedge claim, both README and landing: 'Works best on standard Go/Python/JS/Java/Rust projects with conventional layouts. Mock-heavy code or exotic monorepos may need an explicit domains: block.' Calibrates jagged-frontier expectation before install (Mollick informed-use vs misinformed-use); cuts 'broken tool' perception on first edge case.
